subject: Cloud Computing
title: Virtual Machines in Practice
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Virtual Machines in Practice.

hook.text: You request a server from AWS and it appears in 60 seconds. No human touched any hardware.

buildup.text: A virtual machine is a software-defined computer: you choose its CPU, memory, and storage size.

discovery.text: AWS calls them EC2 instances. Azure says Virtual Machines. Google uses Compute Engine. Same idea.

twist.text: VMs are flexible but wasteful. Most run at 10–20% capacity because users over-provision just in case.

climax.text: Despite newer options like containers, virtual machines still power most cloud workloads today.

punchline.text: Virtual machines: old technology, still the cloud's workhorse.

quiz.question: What is a common inefficiency with cloud virtual machines?
options: Users often over-provision, leaving most capacity unused
Virtual machines can only run one application at a time
They require manual rebooting every 24 hours
